title: Lumeau
description: A shader adding shadows, light and waves, while being lightweight to allow older hardware to run at a good number of fps.
body:
# Description
Lumeau is lighweight shader built to have simple and good visual imitating minecraft color pallette and light color in general but in some cases I added some of my own choice to have a more pleasing look
![forest of autumnal leaves](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/Rosc1kAc/images/ddde376639de9b3f56b118ace4e7f12af995a266.png)
# Features
Lumeau additions:
- Sunlight color will change depending on the sun angle in the sky, this will add red-orange color to blocks when the sun rise or set and more yellow-white color at noon
- Moonlight color is subtle with a white color
- Shadows are clear and crisp, no blur is added by default (can be change in settings menu)
- Waves are simple (no reflexion for now)
- Lighting calculation are a little different from vanilla minecraft with little highlight from the sun and moon
- The end portal have shader changed to look 3D
# Goal
Lumeau has been design to perform well on old hardware because I don't have a high end GPU (for curious person, I have a GTX 960)

**I want Lumeau to be efficient in performance but also having the best looking I can give**
# Installation
**Lumeau use [Sodium](https://modrinth.com/mod/sodium) and [Iris](https://modrinth.com/mod/iris) loader to be run**.
You can install this mod via modrinth.
Download the lastest version of Lumeau then you have to put Lumeau in the shaders folder in your .minecraft and select it in the Iris menu.
